why: own findings post with no incoming question

author: wtgowers
text: use of LLMs may well end up muddying the waters to the point where there is no longer any sensible way of singling out a small number of mathematicians to reward in this way. So it's more of a practical matter than a moral one.

author: wtgowers
text: With hindsight, could this have been done by somebody bothering to do a traditional brute-force computer search, or was AI essential for some more focused and intelligent search? What was the chain of thought like?
draft_reply: i'd want the ablation more than the label: same search space and verifier, once with unguided enumeration and once with the model choosing branches. nodes visited, wall time, and first-certificate time would tell us what the intelligence actually bought.
why: offers a concrete experimental answer to the exact methodological question

why: resolved clarification in an existing exchange

author: khoiiiind
text: At one end is human intent, informally stated. At the other end is machine verification. These two are the only invariants. The space in between will be filled completely by LLMs and LLM-orchestrated processes.
draft_reply: the endpoints look right. i would keep one more invariant in the middle: a durable certificate boundary. models can rewrite every step of the route, but the object handed to the verifier should remain small enough to rerun after the model and its context are gone.
why: adds a concrete certificate-design principle to a theorem-proving forecast

author: lanyon_ai
text: Formally verified charged particle kinetics in 2D to 6D phase space: 12,000 lines of C, 18,500 lines of Lean, 312 theorems, 123 seconds
draft_reply: the interesting denominator is missing: how many of the 312 theorems close against trusted library lemmas with no admitted axioms, and does a clean checkout rebuild both the C artifact and every Lean theorem? that reproducibility boundary would make the 123 seconds interpretable.
why: asks precise, valuable questions about trust and reproducibility of a strong formal-verification claim

author: Surreal_Intel
text: Toward Auditable AI Scientists: Hypothesis Evolution Protocol turns hypothesis generation into a reconstructable process
draft_reply: the useful stress test is a forked replay: start from the same signed state, change one observation, and require the system to expose exactly which hypothesis transitions diverge. auditability becomes much easier to measure when the counterfactual log has a known first difference.
why: proposes a concrete auditability test tailored to the claimed protocol
